description_long: This sculpture depicts a kneeling human figure, likely male, with a serene expression and a traditional headdress reminiscent of ancient Egyptian art. The figure holds a large, inscribed tablet or stela in front of its torso, which features a smaller carved figure in relief and hieroglyphic inscriptions. The arms are bent at the elbows, hands resting on the sides of the tablet, and the figure's legs are folded beneath the body in a formal, upright pose. The base of the sculpture is rectangular and also bears inscriptions, suggesting a commemorative or religious function. The surface appears to be made of dark stone, with a matte, slightly rough finish and visible chisel marks. The overall form is solid and monolithic, with no apparent moving or interactive parts.
description: A kneeling human figure in ancient Egyptian style, holding an inscribed tablet with a smaller figure in relief. The sculpture is carved from dark stone and features detailed inscriptions and a formal, upright pose.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full face, headdress, and the inscribed tablet with a smaller figure in relief.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the head, arm, and the thickness of the tablet.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the headdress and additional inscriptions running vertically down the back.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the figure, arm, and the side of the inscribed base.
attribute_subject_matter: kneeling human figure holding an inscribed tablet in ancient Egyptian style
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
